summary: Plan-only classification for five open PR inventory items. No merge recommendation is emitted because none has complete merge preflight proof with Codex /review. One PR is routed to central security due token-exposure subject matter; three PRs need targeted maintainer judgment or author/review follow-up; one test-only PR is kept independent for normal maintainer review.

actions.reason: Independent, focused test PR with passing checks, but this plan cannot recommend merge without the required merge preflight and Codex /review proof.
The PR appears to involve token/secret exposure handling, so it is out of scope for ProjectClownfish backlog cleanup even though the artifact-level security flag was false.
Active author-followup status blocks automated finalization; maintainer judgment is needed to decide whether the author response is still required before repair or merge review.
The provider/auth compatibility surface and 20-file feature delta are broad enough to require maintainer judgment before any merge or replacement-fix path.
Failing checks plus unresolved bot-review context and a moderate UI feature delta block merge and prevent a concrete repair artifact from being safely planned from the compacted evidence.

needs_human: #92584 must be handled by central OpenClaw security because it appears to involve token/secret exposure behavior.
#92647 needs maintainer judgment on the waiting-on-author state before finalization.
#92649 needs maintainer judgment because the provider/auth compatibility delta is broad for automated finalization.
#92654 needs maintainer judgment or refreshed hydration because failing checks and unresolved bot-review context block merge or repair planning.
